articles.title: Japan Faces a Growing Problem: Where to Store Nuclear Waste?
articles.summary: Japan is restarting some of its nuclear power plants, but scientists and engineers have discovered a serious challenge — the country is almost completely out of room to store used nuclear fuel. Spent fuel rods are highly radioactive and must be kept safe for thousands of years, yet Japan has not yet agreed on a final plan for where to put them. Storage facilities at power stations are nearly full, and no community has volunteered to host a permanent underground waste site. Experts warn that without a long-term solution, Japan's nuclear energy future remains uncertain.

lv1: What kind of waste does Japan need to find a place to store? (日本需要找地方儲存哪一種廢料？)
Why is used nuclear fuel considered dangerous? (為甚麼用過的核燃料被認為是危險的？)
lv2: Why is it so difficult to find a community willing to store nuclear waste underground? (為甚麼很難找到願意在地下儲存核廢料的社區？)
How does the problem of nuclear waste storage affect a country's decision to use nuclear energy? (核廢料儲存問題如何影響一個國家使用核能的決定？)
lv3: Some people believe nuclear energy is clean and necessary to fight climate change, while others say the risks outweigh the benefits. Which side do you agree with, and why? (有人認為核能清潔且有助對抗氣候變化，亦有人認為風險大於益處。你同意哪一方？為甚麼？)

Should governments have the right to force a community to accept a nuclear waste site if no one volunteers? Discuss the tension between national needs and local rights. (如果沒有社區自願接收核廢料，政府是否有權強制要求某社區接受？請討論國家需要與地方權利之間的矛盾。)

articles.title: Hong Kong Trials Hands-Free Smart Border Crossing at HKZM Bridge
articles.summary: Hong Kong's Immigration Department is launching a brand-new type of electronic border crossing called the 'Contactless e-Channel.' Instead of tapping a card or pressing a button, travellers can pass through the gate automatically using facial recognition and other smart sensors — completely hands-free. The first trial will begin on 25 June at the Hong Kong–Zhuhai–Macao Bridge passenger terminal. If successful, the technology could be rolled out to other border crossing points across the city. Officials hope it will make the crossing process faster, more convenient, and more hygienic for everyone.

lv1: Where will Hong Kong's new contactless border gate be tested first? (香港新的無感應式過關閘機首先在哪裡測試？)
What technology does the new e-Channel use to recognise travellers? (新型電子通道使用甚麼技術來辨認旅客？)
lv2: How might a hands-free border crossing system make travel more efficient compared to the current system? (無感應式過關系統與現時系統相比，如何提升旅行效率？)
Why might hygiene be an important reason for introducing contactless border gates, especially after recent years of disease outbreaks? (為甚麼衛生是引入無感應式閘機的重要原因，尤其是在近年疫症爆發之後？)
lv3: Facial recognition technology makes travel faster, but some people worry it threatens personal privacy. How should governments balance convenience and privacy when using such technology at borders? (人臉識別技術令出行更快捷，但有人擔心會威脅個人私隱。政府在邊境使用此類技術時，應如何平衡便利與私隱？)

As smart technology replaces manual checks at borders, what might happen to the jobs of immigration officers? Should society be concerned about this change? (隨著智能技術取代邊境人工檢查，入境事務人員的工作會有甚麼影響？社會是否應該對此感到憂慮？)

articles.title: 港府宣布公務員加薪2%　工會指中低層加幅為十年最低
articles.summary: 香港政府今日宣布，本年度公務員整體薪酬調整幅度為2%。政府表示，今次加薪時考慮了未來的財務承擔及整體經濟環境。不過，有公務員工會批評，這個加幅對中低職級員工來說是近十年來最低的一次，認為未能反映生活成本的實際上升。工會亦擔心，若日後加入更多難以預測的評估指標，薪酬制度會變得更不透明，對公務員不公平。

lv1: 香港政府今年為公務員加薪多少百分比？
哪個群體對這次加薪幅度表示不滿？
lv2: 為甚麼工會認為2%的加薪幅度對中低層公務員不公平？
政府在決定公務員薪酬調整時，需要考慮哪些重要因素？
lv3: 政府需要平衡公共財政和公務員福利，你認為應如何制定一個公平的薪酬調整機制？
若公務員薪酬長期偏低，可能對香港的公共服務質素帶來甚麼影響？社會又應如何回應？

articles.title: 實習女醫生被捕　涉擅用他人帳號查閱病人資料
articles.summary: 香港一名在明愛醫院實習的24歲女醫生，因多項違規行為被即時解僱，並於本月12日遭警方以「不誠實使用電腦」罪名逮捕。事件涉及她涉嫌擅自使用醫院器材為自己照X光、讓男友代為為病人進行內窺鏡檢查，以及使用其他醫生的帳號登入醫療系統查閱病人私隱資料。案件引發社會關注醫護專業操守及醫院內部監管制度是否足夠。

lv1: 這名實習醫生因哪項罪名被警方逮捕？
醫院管理局對這名實習醫生採取了甚麼即時行動？
lv2: 為甚麼未經授權查閱病人的醫療資料是一種嚴重的違規行為？
醫院應如何加強內部監察，防止類似事件再次發生？
lv3: 這宗事件反映了醫護人員的專業操守問題。你認為醫學院在培訓學生時，應如何加強醫德教育？
社交媒體令部分實習生追求知名度，這種風氣會否對醫護專業造成負面影響？社會應如何看待「KOL醫生」現象？
